result.response_content_preview:
THOUGHT:
Reviewing the existing citations, I have covered data quality cascades (Sambasivan), technical debt in ML systems (Sculley), benchmark generalization failures (Recht), and optimizer generalization gaps (Wilson). However, I am missing a citation that addresses **data leakage**, one of the most insidious and common real-world pitfalls in deep learning. Data leakage occurs when information from outside the training dataset is improperly used during model development, leading to drastically inflated validation metrics and catastrophic failures upon deployment. Adding a foundational paper on this topic will allow me to discuss specific leakage concepts, compare flawed validation practices with robust ones, highlight the research gap in automated leakage detection, and suggest future work on rigorous data partitioning and evaluation protocols.
